commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d2dfee87afcb2b68905fd8c30fc58c9aca9580fa)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm` config, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`, and startup/reload/logout gating so provider auth-state prewarm can be disabled or delayed.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrow missing-control claim: current main has an internal prewarm option but no schema/env operator control. No real constrained-host Discord or provider-CLI starvation reproduction was run in this review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer product review for the new gateway config/env surface and contributor real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's real deployment proof.

target.title: fix(gateway): add provider auth prewarm operator controls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider auth prewarm can still be expensive during gateway startup or recovery, and affected operators do not have a supported way to disable or delay it.
- Solution: expose a small gateway-level provider auth prewarm policy with config and env controls.
- What changed: added `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled`, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.delayMs`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`; startup, reload, and logout-triggered rewarm paths now honor the resolved policy.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not claim to fix the underlying event-loop starvation in `warmCurrentProviderAuthState`, Discord READY timing, or OpenAI/Codex route fallback behavior.

## Motivation

This draft PR follows up on the Discord/operator recovery report in #85997. It also relates to the narrower provider-auth startup/prewarm reproduction in #85999.

This draft proposes an operator safety valve while maintainers evaluate the deeper scheduling/caching fix for provider auth prewarm. It is intentionally not marked as closing either issue because the user-facing reports cover broader startup, channel readiness, and auth-provider routing behavior.

AI-assisted: drafted and implemented with Codex; human review and maintainer product feedback are requested before marking ready.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Follow-up to #85997.
- Related to #85999.
- This draft PR does not close either issue; it proposes a scoped mitigation for provider auth prewarm operator control.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: affected deployments can now disable provider auth prewarm or increase its startup delay without patching OpenClaw internals.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu Linux server, latest `origin/main` at the time of this PR.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: targeted gateway/config tests and core typecheck listed below.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 87 tests passed.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 111 tests passed.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` -> passed.
- Observed result after fix: startup prewarm scheduling, reload rewarm, and logout rewarm skip when the config/env policy disables provider auth prewarm; configured delay is used when enabled.
- What was not tested: live Discord restart timing, real provider CLI starvation on a constrained VM, and OpenAI Codex OAuth fallback behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #85999 includes startup prewarm timing logs; #85997 includes redacted Discord/operator-visible errors.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current main already defers provider auth prewarm, but the exposed prewarm controls are internal test/runtime options rather than an operator-facing config surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no schema-covered config/env option to disable or delay provider auth prewarm on deployments where the prewarm path harms startup or channel recovery.
- Contributing context (if known): #85272 narrowed startup ordering by deferring prewarm; #85999 reports that provider auth prewarm can still monopolize startup on a constrained host.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/config/config-misc.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: disabled prewarm policy skips startup/reload/logout rewarm paths, env override disables config-enabled prewarm, and configured delay is honored.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is policy resolution and call-site gating; these tests cover each touched call path without requiring live providers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): startup prewarm ordering coverage existed, but not operator-facing config/env policy.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional gateway config and env controls:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "providerAuthPrewarm": {
      "enabled": false,
      "delayMs": 5000
    }
  }
}
```

`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1` disables provider auth prewarm regardless of config.

Defaults remain unchanged: provider auth prewarm stays enabled with the existing startup delay.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [provider auth prewarm always scheduled]

After:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [resolve config/env policy] -> [schedule or skip provider auth prewarm]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux server
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: provider behavior mocked in tests
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway startup/reload/logout paths; no live Discord run
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic gateway config in tests

### Steps

1. Disable provider auth prewarm through config or `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
2. Start gateway post-attach runtime, reload gateway config, or trigger model auth logout rewarm in tests.
3. Assert provider auth prewarm is not scheduled; assert configured delay is used when enabled.

### Expected

- Disabled policy skips provider auth prewarm.
- Enabled policy keeps default behavior.
- Configured delay controls startup prewarm delay.

### Actual

- Matches expected in targeted tests.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted gateway/config tests and `tsgo:core` after rebasing onto latest `origin/main`.
- Edge cases checked: env override disabling config-enabled prewarm; invalid schema values rejected; reload/logout rewarm paths honor the same policy.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord startup recovery, real constrained-host prewarm timing, and provider OAuth fallback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; operators may opt into the new config/env controls.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: disabling prewarm may make model/provider auth status colder until first use.
  - Mitigation: default behavior remains enabled; the disable path is explicit opt-in for affected operators.
- Risk: this may be considered a product/config surface decision rather than the preferred root fix.
  - Mitigation: opened as draft, scoped as a mitigation, and does not close the linked issues.
target.author: gigio1023

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
